summary.intent: Audit all navigation category agnostic components against Epic #223 spec to verify full implementation correctness, and reorganize the monolithic navigation test files into per-component modules mirroring the existing `input/` pattern.
summary.outcome: Completed a full 3-phase audit across 8 navigation components: split both monolithic test files into per-component modules, synced 6 stale specs to the correct shipped implementations, fixed Steps S1 behavior, implemented TreeView §5 (Lazy Loading) and §6 (Renamable Nodes) variants, added Link and NavigationMenu proptests, and refactored `LoadChildrenFn` from a dead `Rc`-based alias to a proper `Callback` prop. All 10 xci-fast CI steps passed with 2 of 5 planned commits landed.

repo: The `Effect` associated type has an `EffectName: Copy` constraint, which means effects cannot carry data payloads (e.g., `Effect::LoadChildren(Key)` is impossible). Transient context fields (like `pending_load`) or closure-capturing effects via `Callback` are the valid patterns.

Adapter notification callbacks must use `Callback<dyn Fn(...) + Send + Sync>` (wrapping `Arc`), not `Rc<dyn Fn(...)>`. `Rc` is not `Send + Sync` and breaks `StoredValue`/`Signal` storage in the Service layer.

The `input/` directory split is the canonical pattern for per-component test modules: shared strategies live in `mod.rs`, each component owns a `<component>.rs` sibling, root test binary repoints at `<category>/mod.rs`.

The spec snippet-compile harness (`crates/xtask/src/spec/compile_snippets.rs`) only performs syntax parsing (`syn::parse_file`), not type/borrow checking. Blocks can opt out with `rust,no_check`. Spec edits carry low CI risk.

`TreeItemConfig` is the public construction type for `TreeCollection` — `TreeItem` does not implement `CollectionItem` and there is no public `Node<T>` constructor, so runtime child insertion must go through `TreeItemConfig` and reconstruct via `TreeCollection::new`.
PropTest regression files (`proptest-regressions/state-machines.txt`) get polluted by failing test runs during test authoring; must be manually reverted before committing.
The spec was consistently *behind* the implementation across all 8 components — implementations were correct throughout; audit drift was almost entirely spec staleness, not impl bugs.

NavMenu's open-item gate is intentionally permissive before `SetItems` registration (controlled/default values open immediately); proptest invariants asserting `open_item ⊆ items` must be guarded by `items_registered`.

Link's `pressed` field can legitimately persist into `State::Focused` (e.g., after `[Press, Focus{is_keyboard:false}]`) — the invariant `pressed ⟺ State::Pressed` is wrong; correct is `State::Pressed ⟹ pressed` and `State::Idle ⟹ !pressed`.

code.finding: `Effect::LoadChildren` must be a unit `Copy` variant. The key travels via `pending_load: Option<Key>` in Context (set alongside `load_state[key]=Loading`) or via closure capture in an `on_load_children: Option<Callback<dyn Fn(Key) + Send + Sync>>` prop. After the refactor, the Prop approach is the landed pattern.

The `NextStep` handler on the last step left status as `Current` and fired only `Effect::Complete` — spec says last step should be marked `Status::Complete`. The old test `next_step_completion_preserves_current_status` was actively asserting the wrong behavior. Fixed per user decision.

`TreeItemConfig<T>` needed `#[derive(Clone, PartialEq)]` added (conditional on `T`) so it can ride in the `Clone + PartialEq` `Event` enum for the `ChildrenLoaded { children: Vec<TreeItemConfig<TreeItem>> }` payload.

A leftover `_locale_import_marker(_: Locale)` function referencing a removed import caused a compile error in the proptest binary. The `Locale` import and marker were both dead code from an earlier cleanup pass.

Two silent-contract bugs existed in the spec: controlled `on_page_change` fired with `*ctx.page.get()` (the stale value) instead of `target`, and next/prev/first/last never routed through the callback at all. The impl was correct; both bugs were spec-only.

workflow: When creating clean commits out of already-staged content that spans multiple logical phases, `git reset` → stage file subsets → commit is the right flow; trying to split from a fully-staged tree causes cross-phase contamination.

For test invariants in proptests, first verify the exact machine behavior under edge sequences (e.g., `[Press, Focus]`) before asserting bidirectional equivalences — one-directional implications are safer starting points.

When spec code mentions adapter-side callbacks as `pub type Fn = Rc<dyn Fn(...)>`, cross-check against the workspace's established callback convention before propagating — `Rc` is consistently the wrong choice in this codebase.

The 'best outcome' rule for spec/impl drift (sync whichever is worse to whichever is better, flag material API changes before committing) is an effective default that avoided all 'both wrong' situations in this audit.

Delegating per-component drift analysis to parallel background agents (6 agents for 6 spec files) while doing mechanical work in the foreground is an effective parallelism pattern, but all agent output must be cross-checked against actual impl symbols before trusting.

friction: Creating clean commit slices from a fully-staged working tree required temporarily reverting C4 content (TreeView variant test additions) in C1 files and restoring them afterward — a manual and error-prone dance.

The TreeView `Effect: Copy` constraint wasn't discovered until implementation time, invalidating the initial `Effect::LoadChildren(Key)` design and requiring a full architecture pivot mid-implementation.
The spec §5.1 for TreeView was self-contradictory (§5.1 said callbacks are adapter-layer only, §5.4 step 3 said adapter calls a `load_children` prop) — required judgment to resolve.

PropTest regression file `proptest-regressions/state-machines.txt` was silently polluted by failure seeds from the invariant-authoring iteration and had to be manually caught and reverted before committing.

The `Locale` dead-code leftover in `navigation_menu.rs` wasn't caught until the TreeView subagent reported a compile error in the proptest binary — a cross-file coupling problem in a parallel workstream's file.

Iterating on proptest invariants required multiple compile/run cycles to discover that `pressed ⟺ Pressed` and `open_item ⊆ items` were both over-strong — the machine behavior is subtler than the intuitive model.
open_items: PR not yet opened at transcript end — C1 and C2 committed, C3-C5 (impl fixes, TreeView variants, new proptests) still unstaged.
The `waiting-for-codex-review` loop needs to run after the PR is opened; Epic #223 should only be closed after merge + green CI + Codex approval.

TreeView adapter implementations (Leptos, Dioxus) for §5/§6 variants don't exist yet — the adapter specs were updated but no adapter code exists to implement `on_load_children` wiring or rename-input rendering.

MD060 table-alignment warnings in adapter spec files (`spec/leptos-components/navigation/tree-view.md`, `spec/dioxus-components/navigation/tree-view.md`) introduced by column-width changes — cosmetic and ungated but left unresolved.
TreeView `on_node_keydown_at`, `clamp_focus` ancestor-walk, and DnD guard branches remain uncovered in llvm-cov (pre-existing, not new code).
The `snapshot-reviewed` label needs to be applied to the PR for the new rename-input snapshot fixture.
